question: Due to excessive sand and gravel, the motorcycle rider must not drive

answerOptions.text: along the middle of the lane.
near the left edge of the right lane.
near the right edge of the left lane.
along the side of the pavement.

chosenAnswer: along the middle of the lane.
correctAnswer: along the side of the pavement.
explanation: Sand and gravel usually collect along the side of the pavement.

question: To keep a motorcycle in good working condition, what formula must be followed by the driver every day?
explanation: Before starting the motorcycle, every motorcycle rider must perform a daily check-up by following a 10-point formula.

question: Incorrect use of gears can result in accidents when

answerOptions.text: downshifting, turning, or starting on hills.
riding with a passenger on two-way roads.

chosenAnswer: downshifting, turning, or starting on hills.
correctAnswer: downshifting, turning, or starting on hills.
explanation: There is more to shifting gears than simply getting the motorcycle to accelerate smoothly. Accidents can occur if the gears are used incorrectly, when downshifting, turning, or starting on hills.

question: If you are a fully licensed driver 21 years of age and over and if you register a BAC ____ , you will receive an immediate seven-day licence suspension for a first offence.
explanation: If you register a BAC from .05 to .08 (known as the “warn range”), you will receive an immediate driver’s licence suspension. For a first occurrence, you will be suspended for seven days.

question: How much stopping power is provided by the rear brake?

answerOptions.text: Three quarters of total stopping power
One quarter of total stopping power

chosenAnswer: Three quarters of total stopping power
correctAnswer: One quarter of total stopping power
explanation: Only one quarter of the total stopping power is provided by the rear brake.

question: When a motorcycle rider approaches a railway crossing and the signal lights are flashing, what should s/he do?

answerOptions.text: Stop 5 metres away from the nearest track.
Stop the motorcycle 15 metres away from the nearest track.
Cross the rail track with a speed of 80 km/h.
Stop 12 metres from the farthest track.

chosenAnswer: Stop 5 metres away from the nearest track.
correctAnswer: Stop 5 metres away from the nearest track.
explanation: Motorcyclists must maintain 5-metres' distance from the nearest railway track at railway crossings.

question: If a motorcycle rider wishes to stop his/her vehicle, while stopping, s/he must watch for traffic
explanation: When a rider wants to stop his/her motorcycle, s/he must watch the traffic behind before taking the next steps.

question: Alcohol can impair the driver's ability to
explanation: Alcohol impairs the driver's ability to control, turn, or stop the motorcycle.

question: The motorcyclist should avoid driving in which part of the lane when it is raining?
explanation: The centre part of the lane is more slippery due to oil and grease buildup. Motorcyclists should avoid that particular area in wet conditions.

question: In which of the following situations will the motorcycle rider need to shift his/her weight to the rear end?

answerOptions.text: When the motorcycle loses its balance

explanation: If a front tire blows, the motorcyclist must try to steer straight and shift his/her weight to the rear to control the vehicle.

question: When a motorcycle rider wishes to enter a highway, s/he must

answerOptions.text: take a special route for motorcycles.
enter into the access path and then the merging area.
enter with the lowest possible speed.

chosenAnswer: take a special route for motorcycles.
correctAnswer: enter into the access path and then the merging area.
explanation: Motorcycle riders must enter into the access path and then into the acceleration zone and slowly merge into highway traffic.

question: The taillights of other vehicles are useful to motorists

answerOptions.text: to estimate the distance of the vehicle.
to avoid blind spots of the vehicle.
to see the bumps or rough pavement.
to judge the speed of the vehicle.

chosenAnswer: to estimate the distance of the vehicle.
correctAnswer: to see the bumps or rough pavement.
explanation: The vehicle's taillights can alert the motorist of any rough conditions of the road.

question: Motorcycle riders must not ride their vehicles
explanation: The motorcycle rider should never ride between two lanes, as each lane is designed for only one vehicle.

question: Which is a safer gear to use while going down a hill?
explanation: Using a lower gear while going down a hill acts as a brake.

question: When a motorcycle bounces, the motorcycle rider can be protected from immediate danger by

answerOptions.text: removing his/her feet from the footpegs and balancing them.
dragging the feet along the ground.
bending the body into the proper direction.

chosenAnswer: removing his/her feet from the footpegs and balancing them.
explanation: The driver must hold the handgrips firmly so as to not lose grip if the motorcycle bounces.

question: The area slightly to the left or the right of the centre of the lane is called the
explanation: This area is called the tire track, because all tires would travel in this area.

question: Motorcycle riders must maintain the following distance from other vehicles
explanation: Motorcycle riders must maintain at least a 2-to-3-seconds' safe following distance from other vehicles.

question: A large seat and separate footrests are required on the motorcycle for
explanation: The motorcycle must be equipped with a larger seat and separate footrests for a passenger.

question: The motorcycle rider must check his/her mirrors every
explanation: Motorcycle riders must check their mirrors every 5-7 seconds to watch the traffic behind them.

question: One of the causes of collisions at intersections is a vehicle making a left turn across your path. To cut down your chances of being hit,
explanation: A vehicle making a left turn across your path is one of the leading causes of collisions at intersections. To cut down your chances of being hit, approach slowly and move to the right.

question: The motorcycle rider must use the following for correct stopping

answerOptions.text: Front brake first and rear brake next

chosenAnswer: Front brake first and rear brake next
explanation: Always use both brakes simultaneously to decrease the stopping distance.

question: Perfect balance can be maintained by motorcycle riders by holding the knees firmly against the gas tank while
explanation: In order to maintain balance, every motorcycle rider must hold his/her knees firmly against the gas tank while making turns.

question: What is the purpose of using a helmet while riding a motorcycle?

answerOptions.text: To protect the motorcyclist from rain and snow
To function as a balance for the motorcycle
To protect the motorcyclist from sun rays
To protect the motorcyclist from head injuries in case of a crash

chosenAnswer: To protect the motorcyclist from rain and snow
correctAnswer: To protect the motorcyclist from head injuries in case of a crash
explanation: As per Ontario law, every motorcycle rider and passenger must wear a proper helmet while moving on a motorcycle. It provides protection from head injuries during a collision.

question: The minimum age limit for applicants of a motorcycle driving licence is
explanation: The minimum age requirement for the learner's licence is 16 years.

question: Which of the following will affect motorcycle riders more than four-wheel vehicle drivers?
explanation: Motorcycle riders are adversely affected by road and weather changes as they are not protected like four-wheel vehicle drivers are.

question: When entering a curve, you should

answerOptions.text: look as far around it as possible.
reduce your speed before the curve.
turn your head, not your shoulders, in the direction of the curve.

chosenAnswer: look as far around it as possible.
explanation: As you enter the curve, look as far around it as possible. This helps you stay in a smooth line around the curve. If you look only at the road directly in front of you, you are likely to wander back and forth across the lane, constantly correcting your steering. On a curve with a short sight distance, drive in the tire track where you can see more of the road ahead. If the curve is to the left, use the right tire track. If the curve is to the right, keep as far left as possible while watching for oncoming traffic that might be cutting the curve short. You may also change tire tracks in order to drive through a more gradual curve than if you followed the full curve of the road.

question: Class M1 licensed motorcyclists driving at night should do the following to prevent accidents

answerOptions.text: Class M1 licence holders are not allowed to drive at night.
Maintain considerable distance from other vehicles

chosenAnswer: Class M1 licence holders are not allowed to drive at night.
correctAnswer: Class M1 licence holders are not allowed to drive at night.
explanation: Class M1 licence holders are not allowed to drive at night due to their insufficient experience.

question: What should the driver do when the motorcycle chain locks the rear wheel?

answerOptions.text: Apply brakes to stop the vehicle immediately.
Maintain control and pull over immediately.
Turn off and turn on the engine.

chosenAnswer: Apply brakes to stop the vehicle immediately.
correctAnswer: Maintain control and pull over immediately.
explanation: When the chain locks the rear wheel, maintain control and pull over immediately.

question: Which of the following three documents are required for motorcycle riders in Ontario?

answerOptions.text: Birth certificate, marriage certificate, and valid driver's licence
Vehicle ownership document, passport, and insurance liability card
Valid driver's licence, birth certificate, and passport
Insurance liability card, valid driver's licence, and vehicle ownership document

chosenAnswer: Birth certificate, marriage certificate, and valid driver's licence
correctAnswer: Insurance liability card, valid driver's licence, and vehicle ownership document
explanation: Insurance liability card, valid driver's licence, and vehicle ownership documents are required for all drivers in Ontario.

question: The three principles used when braking hard in a turn are
explanation: When braking hard in a turn, the driver must follow the principles of "straighten, square, squeeze" and practice them.

question: When a motorcycle rider wants to turn while moving faster than 25 km/h, s/he must use the
explanation: When travelling at speeds greater than 25 km/h, the rider must push on the handgrip in the direction of the turn.

question: When a motorist reaches a through intersection where s/he may not need to stop, s/he must

answerOptions.text: stop in front of four-wheeled vehicles.
pass with maximum speed to avoid collisions.
yield to pedestrians and vehicles or come to a complete stop if necessary.

chosenAnswer: stop in front of four-wheeled vehicles.
correctAnswer: yield to pedestrians and vehicles or come to a complete stop if necessary.
explanation: Whenever a motorist reaches a through intersection, s/he must yield to pedestrians and other vehicles as per the right-of-way rules.

question: To protect the face and eyes from flying material, the motorcyclist should wear
explanation: Face protective shields protect the motorcyclist's face and eyes from flying objects.

question: High speed wobble for a motorcycle can be prevented

answerOptions.text: by further increasing the speed of the vehicle.
by suddenly applying brakes and balancing the vehicle.
by shifting the weight of the body to one side.
by slowly closing the throttle without applying brakes.

chosenAnswer: by further increasing the speed of the vehicle.
correctAnswer: by slowly closing the throttle without applying brakes.
explanation: High speed wobble can be prevented by slowly closing the throttle and reducing the speed without applying the brakes.

question: While making a turn, the passenger on a motorcycle must

answerOptions.text: lean in the direction of the turn.
lean in the opposite direction of the turn.

correctAnswer: lean in the direction of the turn.
explanation: The driver as well as the passenger must lean in the direction of the turn.

question: Which of the following actions can save a motorcycle rider's life when s/he wants to change lanes, position within a lane, or before turning off the roadway?

answerOptions.text: A quick movement toward the other lane

chosenAnswer: A quick movement toward the other lane
explanation: The driver should do a quick shoulder check before changing the lane position, position within a lane, or before turning off the roadway.

question: Motorcycle riders must give the right-of-way to
explanation: Motorcycle riders must stop for pedestrians at crosswalks or intersections.

question: To make a safe turn, the motorcyclist must

answerOptions.text: lean the bike in the direction of the turn.
lean the bike against the direction of the turn.

correctAnswer: lean the bike in the direction of the turn.
explanation: To turn a motorcycle safely, the rider must lean the bike in the direction of the turn.

question: Which of the following is most useful to the motorcycle rider to help him/her maintain balance?

answerOptions.text: Keeping the feet steady on the footpegs

correctAnswer: Keeping the feet steady on the footpegs
explanation: Motorcycle riders must keep their feet firmly on the footpegs. This will help them maintain balance.

question: For safe driving on the road, a driver must do the following

answerOptions.text: Apply brakes often to prevent any collisions.
Check the surroundings and scan the road in all directions.
Turn the headlights on to improve vision.
Change lanes frequently to avoid any accidents with fast-moving vehicles.

chosenAnswer: Apply brakes often to prevent any collisions.
correctAnswer: Check the surroundings and scan the road in all directions.
explanation: The driver must watch the surrounding areas and scan the road in all directions to prevent accidents.

question: When riding, you should

answerOptions.text: keep your eyes scanning the road ahead, behind and beside you.
look at the road right in front of your motorcycle.
fix your gaze on the vehicle ahead.

chosenAnswer: keep your eyes scanning the road ahead, behind and beside you.
correctAnswer: keep your eyes scanning the road ahead, behind and beside you.
explanation: Keep your eyes constantly moving and scanning the road ahead, beside and behind. Don't look at any one place for more than two seconds; trouble could be developing where you aren't looking.

question: When entering or leaving a highway, a group of motorcyclists should drive
explanation: Staggered formation can be safely used on an open road. However, you should drive single file (in a single line) on curves, in turns or when entering or leaving a freeway.

question: Which of the following statements about carrying passengers are true?

answerOptions.text: It will take you longer to speed up.
It will take you longer to make a turn.
All of the above statements are true.

correctAnswer: All of the above statements are true.
explanation: When you carry cargo or a passenger on your motorcycle, the stopping distance will increase. It will also take you longer to speed up or turn. Adjust your riding accordingly.

question: A steady yellow traffic light at an intersection indicates that you must

answerOptions.text: increase your speed to get past the yellow light before it turns red.
stop if you can do so safely.
slow down and proceed with caution.
stop, yield to pedestrians, and proceed with caution.

chosenAnswer: increase your speed to get past the yellow light before it turns red.
correctAnswer: stop if you can do so safely.
explanation: A steady yellow traffic light indicates that the traffic light is about to turn red. Stop if it is safe to do so. However, don't stop suddenly and risk getting rear-ended by the vehicle behind you. If you cannot stop safely, proceed with caution into the intersection, perform the maneuver you intended to make there, and clear the intersection. Never speed up in an attempt to "beat the light" before it turns red.

question: When upshifting, you should

answerOptions.text: twist the throttle with the clutch pulled in.
twist the clutch while you pull in the throttle.
twist the throttle as you pull in the clutch.

correctAnswer: twist the throttle as you pull in the clutch.
explanation: To shift up, twist the throttle as you pull in the clutch. Move the shift lever up with your toe until it stops. When you can hear and feel the gear engage, ease off on the clutch and slowly twist the throttle back up to speed.

question: When carrying cargo, it is important to

answerOptions.text: weigh it before loading it on the motorcycle.
secure it just to the sides of the motorcycle.

chosenAnswer: weigh it before loading it on the motorcycle.
explanation: When carrying cargo, it is important to keep the load low to maintain a low centre of gravity.

question: The best way to discourage other vehicles from trying to share your lane is to

answerOptions.text: ride a little to the left or right of the centre of your lane.
ride on the line that divides the traffic lanes.
ride to the far right of your lane.
ride to the far left of your lane.

chosenAnswer: ride a little to the left or right of the centre of your lane.
correctAnswer: ride a little to the left or right of the centre of your lane.
explanation: To discourage other vehicles from sharing your lane, it is best to ride a little to the left or right of the centre of your lane, in the track where the tires of a four-wheeled vehicle would travel. This spot is referred to as the left or right “tire track.” It is also called the “blocking position” because it blocks or discourages other drivers from sharing your lane.

question: You are following a large truck that is signaling a right turn. You should anticipate

answerOptions.text: the truck turning close to the right curb.

explanation: When any vehicle turns, its rear wheels follow a shorter path than its front wheels. The longer the vehicle, the greater the difference in the turning path. If a large truck (big rig) tries to turn right from the right lane, it might overrun the right curb. This is why big rigs must often swing wide left to complete a right turn. When you follow a big rig, look at its turn signals before you try to pass it. If the truck appears to be turning left, check its turn signals; the driver may be turning right but first swinging wide.

question: While riding a motorcycle, you should position your feet

answerOptions.text: to drag lightly on the ground.

explanation: While riding a motorcycle, you should position your feet firmly on the footrests. This helps maintain balance. Pointing your feet downward could cause them to catch on something.

question: When riding with a passenger, how should you adjust your riding?

answerOptions.text: Increase the throttle when making turns.
Go slower when taking curves or corners.
Make sure the gas tank is at least half full.

correctAnswer: Go slower when taking curves or corners.
explanation: Your motorcycle will respond more slowly with a passenger. Hence, you should ride a bit slower, especially when taking curves, corners or bumps.

question: In countersteering, to initiate a turn, PUSHING the right handgrip will allow the motorcycle to
explanation: In counter-steering, you momentarily steer counter to the direction in which you want to turn. This causes the motorcycle to lean in the direction for the turn.

question: When riding in a group, beginner riders should be positioned

answerOptions.text: at the end of the group.
at the front of the group.

explanation: When riding in a group, an inexperienced rider should be positioned right behind the leader. The inexperienced rider won't have to chase after the group, and more experienced riders will be able to keep an eye on the inexperienced rider from behind.

question: What type of group formation should be used when riding through curves?
explanation: When riding through curves, it is best to ride single file (in a single line).

question: If the traffic signal light facing you is red and you intend to go straight through the intersection, what must you do?

answerOptions.text: Slow down; then proceed when the way is clear.
Come to a complete stop and wait for the light to turn green.
Stop if there are vehicles on your path of travel.
Stop momentarily; then proceed when safe.

chosenAnswer: Slow down; then proceed when the way is clear.
correctAnswer: Come to a complete stop and wait for the light to turn green.
explanation: When the traffic light is red, you must come to a complete stop. Proceed only when the signal turns green and the way is clear.

question: If you are riding over an obstacle on the road, what is the best way to absorb shock?
explanation: If you are riding over an obstacle on the road, the best way to absorb shock is to raise yourself slightly off the seat and let your knees and elbows absorb the shock.

question: When carrying cargo or a passenger,

answerOptions.text: start slowing down earlier than usual.
maintain a greater following distance between you and the vehicle ahead.

explanation: When carrying cargo or a passenger, some speed adjustments are required. Drive slower and leave more space ahead and to your sides. Adjust your riding accordingly.

question: You must have your motorcycle's headlight turned on
explanation: All motorcycles must have a white light at the front (headlight) and a red light at the back (rear or tail light), and these must be used at all times of the day and night.

question: At night, if you meet another vehicle with blinding bright lights, the safest thing to do is

answerOptions.text: look at the headlights of the approaching vehicle.
open and close your eyes rapidly.
look slightly to the right-hand side.

correctAnswer: look slightly to the right-hand side.
explanation: At night, should you meet another vehicle with blinding bright lights, the safest thing to do is to look slightly to the right-hand side.

question: When riding in a group, motorcyclists should usually ride
explanation: When riding in a group, motorcyclists should usually ride in a staggered formation. It keeps the group together while still maintaining an adequate space cushion.

question: When driving in a group, group members should communicate by
explanation: When you're driving in a group, communicate with hand signals. Make sure all group members are familiar with the hand signals before you start your trip. To speak to another driver, wait until the two of you have stopped.

question: This driver's hand signal indicates that this driver intends to
explanation: This driver intends to slow down or stop.

question: When you are being passed by another vehicle, you should

answerOptions.text: move as far to the right as possible.
move toward the left of your lane.
move toward the centre of your lane.

chosenAnswer: move as far to the right as possible.
correctAnswer: move toward the centre of your lane.
explanation: When you're being passed by an oncoming vehicle or from behind, move toward the centre of your lane. This way, you won't be too close to a passing vehicle and you'll discourage other drivers from moving into your lane.

question: When crossing a very slippery surface, you should

answerOptions.text: pull in the clutch and coast.
stop and walk the bike across.

chosenAnswer: pull in the clutch and coast.
correctAnswer: pull in the clutch and coast.
explanation: When crossing a very slippery surface, you should pull in the clutch and coast across.

question: Why are gravel roads potentially dangerous to motorcyclists?

answerOptions.text: They do not have posted speed limits.
Other drivers are known to drive recklessly.
Gravel provides less traction than pavement.

chosenAnswer: They do not have posted speed limits.
correctAnswer: Gravel provides less traction than pavement.
explanation: Gravel roads are potentially dangerous to motorcyclists because the loose sand and gravel provide little traction.

question: When can you ride a motorcycle between two lanes of traffic?

answerOptions.text: Only if both lanes are headed in the same direction

chosenAnswer: Only if both lanes are headed in the same direction
explanation: A motorcycle needs a full lane width to maneuver safely. A motorcycle should never be ridden between lanes of traffic, even if it looks like there is room. It could put you too close to other vehicles; if they were to start making even a small movement such as changing lanes, you would have no place else to go.

question: Which of the following statements about driving in the rain is FALSE?

answerOptions.text: Use the front brake if you need to stop quickly.
Slow down and avoid sudden moves.
Avoid driving in the centre of the road.
Wear bright colours and reflective or fluorescent clothing.

chosenAnswer: Use the front brake if you need to stop quickly.
correctAnswer: Use the front brake if you need to stop quickly.
explanation: Both brakes together are more effective than the rear brake alone, even on a slippery surface.

question: Snow removal vehicles on public roadways are equipped with flashing ______ lights.
explanation: Snow-removal vehicles on public roadways are equipped with flashing blue lights that can be seen from up to 150 metres.

question: The edge of the road is dangerous for motorcycles because

answerOptions.text: the pavement is of poor quality.
dirt and gravel provide little traction.
it is not driven on often.

chosenAnswer: the pavement is of poor quality.
correctAnswer: dirt and gravel provide little traction.
explanation: The side of the road can be dangerous to motorcycles because the dirt and gravel that build up there provide little traction.

question: Most of your motorcycle's stopping power is provided by

answerOptions.text: the front and rear brakes equally.

explanation: The front brake provides three-quarters of your motorcycle's stopping power.

question: For increased safety when riding at night, you should

answerOptions.text: watch the taillights of the vehicle ahead.

explanation: For increased safety when riding at night, you should do the following: 1. Reduce your speed to give yourself more time to react to the unexpected. 2. Increase your following distance to give yourself more room to maneuver if the vehicle ahead of you stops short. 3. Watch the vehicle ahead of you. Its headlights can help give you a better view of the road. And if the vehicle's taillights seem to bounce up and down, it is likely riding over bumps.

question: Which of the following statements about blind spots is FALSE?

answerOptions.text: Larger vehicles have smaller blind spots.
There are blind spots to the left rear and right rear of a vehicle.
The best way to compensate for blind spots is to turn and glance over your shoulder.
Don't drive in another vehicle's blind spot.

chosenAnswer: Larger vehicles have smaller blind spots.
correctAnswer: Larger vehicles have smaller blind spots.
explanation: Every vehicle has blind spots. Generally, the larger the vehicle, the larger the blind spots.

question: When approaching a Yield sign, what does the law require you to do?

answerOptions.text: Slow down; stop if necessary to yield the right-of-way.
Slow down and proceed through the intersection slowly.
Slow down and stop even if the intersection is clear.

chosenAnswer: Slow down; stop if necessary to yield the right-of-way.
correctAnswer: Slow down; stop if necessary to yield the right-of-way.
explanation: This sign means that drivers must slow down and be ready to stop if necessary to let any vehicle or pedestrian pass.

question: M1 licence holders may NOT drive

answerOptions.text: on country roads unless accompanied by a driving instructor.
on rural roads with speed limits below 80 km/h.
on most roads whose speed limits are over 80 km/h.

correctAnswer: on most roads whose speed limits are over 80 km/h.
explanation: While you hold an M1 licence, you may not drive on roads whose speed limits are over 80 km/h, except highways 11, 17, 61, 69, 71, 101, 102, 144 and 655. (These exceptions are made because some drivers have no other route available to them.) Also, you may not drive from one-half hour after sunset to one-half hour before sunrise.

question: To carry cargo, you should
explanation: To carry a load, you should position it forward and low and distribute the weight evenly. Keep your motorcycle balanced and keep its centre of gravity low.

question: This hand signal indicates that the driver intends to
explanation: This hand signal indicates that the driver intends to turn right.

question: Under ideal conditions, you should maintain a following distance of at least _______ behind the vehicle ahead of you.
explanation: Always keep at least a two-second following distance behind the vehicle in front of you. This gives you time to react if something happens ahead. It also gives you a better view of things in the road, such as potholes, slippery spots, chunks of tire tread, or debris. If conditions are less than ideal, such as in bad weather, leave even more space.

question: Before making a left turn from a one-way street, you should position your motorcycle

answerOptions.text: as close to the left side of the roadway as possible.
as close to the right side of the road as possible.
to the right of the white line.
to the left of the white line.

chosenAnswer: as close to the left side of the roadway as possible.
correctAnswer: as close to the left side of the roadway as possible.
explanation: When making a left turn, position your motorcycle as close to the left side of the roadway as possible.

question: On a two-lane road where traffic moves in opposite directions, you may pass on the left only when

answerOptions.text: a solid yellow line is next to your lane.
a broken yellow line is not next to your lane.
a broken yellow line is next to your lane.
a double solid yellow line is in the centre.

chosenAnswer: a solid yellow line is next to your lane.
correctAnswer: a broken yellow line is next to your lane.
explanation: On a two-lane road where traffic moves in opposite directions, you may pass on the left when a broken yellow line is next to your lane.

question: What type of clothing is best for daytime riding?

answerOptions.text: Any clothing that covers your arms and legs
Gray clothing to blend into the environment

explanation: Most collisions occur in broad daylight. Wear brightly colored clothing to increase your chances of being seen. Bright orange, red, yellow, or green jackets or vests are best for being seen. Reflective material on your vest and helmets will help drivers see you from the side.

question: A driver in front of you is signaling with his or her hand and arm pointing upward. This driver intends to
explanation: You may use your vehicle's signal lights or hand signals. If the signal lights are not working or if bright sunlight makes them hard to see, use hand signals. A driver in front of you who signals with his or her hand and arm pointing upward is about to turn right.

question: You should not drive your motorcycle alongside cars because

answerOptions.text: you might be in a blind spot.
a car driver might not be able to turn.
a car driver's visibility might be reduced.

correctAnswer: you might be in a blind spot.
explanation: It can be dangerous to drive alongside cars because you might be riding in their blind spots.

question: A centre lane that is marked on each side by a solid yellow line and a broken yellow line is reserved for _________ and can be used by vehicles travelling in either direction.
explanation: This is a shared centre lane, also known as a shared left-turn lane. Vehicles in either direction may use this lane only to make a left turn (or a U-turn where permitted). Use this lane with caution because vehicles travelling in the opposite direction may use it, too.

question: If a large animal suddenly runs in front of your motorcycle, you should

answerOptions.text: do your best to swerve around it.
brake as hard as you can.

chosenAnswer: do your best to swerve around it.
correctAnswer: do your best to swerve around it.
explanation: If you cannot stop in time and the animal is large, such as a dog or moose, you may have no choice but to swerve; if you are in traffic, try to stay in your lane. However, if the animal is small, you have a better chance of surviving an impact with it than with another vehicle.

question: The rear brake is controlled by
explanation: The rear brake is controlled by the right foot pedal. The front brake is controlled by the right hand lever.

question: You had to make an emergency stop. How can you notify other drivers that you need help?

answerOptions.text: Put your helmet on the ground near the road.
Honk your horn once in a while.
Stand on the shoulder of the road and wave to passing drivers.

chosenAnswer: Put your helmet on the ground near the road.
correctAnswer: Put your helmet on the ground near the road.
explanation: If you must make an emergency stop, pull your motorcycle off the road as far as possible. If you need help, place your helmet on the ground near the road. This is a signal among motorcycle drivers that a motorcyclist needs help.

question: If a dog starts to chase you while you are riding, you should

answerOptions.text: stop until the owner is found.
approach the animal and then speed away.

correctAnswer: approach the animal and then speed away.
explanation: If a dog starts to chase you, you should approach the animal and then speed away. This should stop the dog from chasing you.

question: What must you do at a flashing red traffic signal?

answerOptions.text: Proceed with caution; the traffic signal is out of order.
Stop and do not proceed until the signal stops flashing.
Stop and yield as you would at a stop sign.
Proceed straight through; you cannot turn left or right at a flashing red signal.

chosenAnswer: Proceed with caution; the traffic signal is out of order.
correctAnswer: Stop and yield as you would at a stop sign.
explanation: You must treat a flashing red signal like a stop sign. That is, you must stop before entering the intersection or crosswalk, yield to traffic and pedestrians, and then proceed when it is safe to do so.

question: The best way to deal with a tailgater is to
explanation: If you are being tailgated, try to get the tailgater in front of you. (You are safer with the tailgater in front of you than right behind you.) Change lanes if possible, slow down, and encourage the tailgater behind you to pass. Don't increase your speed in an attempt to appease or outdistance the tailgater. No speed is too fast for some tailgaters.

question: What do these pavement markings mean?

answerOptions.text: Vehicles travelling in either direction can make left turns.
Vehicles travelling in either direction can make right turns.

chosenAnswer: Vehicles travelling in either direction can make left turns.
correctAnswer: Vehicles travelling in either direction can make left turns.
explanation: This is a shared centre lane, also known as a shared left-turn lane. Vehicles in either direction may use this lane only to make a left turn (or a U-turn where permitted). Use this lane with caution because vehicles travelling in the opposite direction may use it, too.

question: How can you stop quickly on a slippery road?

answerOptions.text: Stop braking and coast to a stop.

explanation: Pull in the clutch and apply both brakes quickly and smoothly but without locking the wheels (or without ABS kicking in if your motorcycle has ABS brakes). If either wheel locks, release the brake momentarily to get the wheel rolling, then re-apply the brakes but not to the point of locking. This is called threshold braking.

question: If you are approaching an intersection at which the traffic lights are not working, you should

answerOptions.text: treat the intersection like an all-way stop.
yield to the traffic on your right.
stop, yield to cross traffic, and then proceed.
slow down and proceed with caution.

chosenAnswer: treat the intersection like an all-way stop.
correctAnswer: treat the intersection like an all-way stop.
explanation: During an electrical failure, traffic lights at intersections will not work. Proceed cautiously and use the intersection the same way you would use an intersection with all-way stop signs.

question: To maintain adequate control of your motorcycle, your arms should be positioned

answerOptions.text: with elbows slightly bent and wrists low.

correctAnswer: with elbows slightly bent and wrists low.
explanation: To maintain adequate control of the motorcycle, your arms should be positioned with elbows slightly bent and wrists low to prevent you from increasing your speed by mistake.

question: What does a double solid yellow line in the centre of a two-lane road mean?

answerOptions.text: Passing is permitted during the day.

explanation: A double solid yellow line in the centre of a two-lane road means that no passing is allowed on either side.

question: You are riding along the road and get an insect smeared on your face shield that obscures your view. What should you do?

answerOptions.text: Stop immediately and clear off your face shield.
Pull up the face shield and continue driving.
Speed up to get to your destination faster.
Pull off the road when it is safe and clear off your face shield.

chosenAnswer: Stop immediately and clear off your face shield.
correctAnswer: Pull off the road when it is safe and clear off your face shield.
explanation: From time to time, insects, cigarette butts thrown from other vehicles or stones kicked up by the tires of the vehicle ahead may hit you. If you are not wearing face protection, you could be hit in the eye or the face. If you are wearing face protection, it could become smeared or cracked, making it difficult to see. Whatever happens, do not let it affect your control of your motorcycle or moped. Keep your eyes on the road and your hands on the handlebars. As soon as it is safe, pull off the road and repair the damage.

question: To cross a railway crossing safely, you should

answerOptions.text: look both ways only if the signals are flashing.
do it quickly but back up if you notice a train.
first park within 10 feet of the crossing.
look both ways for an approaching train and listen carefully.

chosenAnswer: look both ways only if the signals are flashing.
correctAnswer: look both ways for an approaching train and listen carefully.
explanation: Look both ways as you approach a railway crossing, even if the flashing signal is not functioning. Never stop your vehicle when any part of your vehicle is on the track. When in traffic, never proceed across the tracks unless the opposite side has adequate room for the full length of your vehicle. Never drive around lowered gates or fail to heed a warning signal, even if you can’t see a train approaching. Also keep in mind that trains are at least two metres wider than the tracks that they run on, so you don't want to be too close.

question: The front brake is operated by
explanation: The front brake is operated by the right hand lever. The left hand lever controls the clutch. The right foot pedal is for the rear brake, and the left foot lever is the gear shifter.

question: Weaving when riding over a metal grating

answerOptions.text: is caused by poor motorcycle skills.

explanation: Weaving caused by riding over a metal grating is usually not hazardous. Relax and stay on course.

question: Tire pressure ________ at lower temperatures.
explanation: Checking tire pressure is recommended every time you drive. Proper tire pressure means safer driving and longer-lasting tires. Use an air-pressure gauge to get accurate readings. Keep in mind that tire pressures decrease when the tires are cold.

question: When starting on a hill, you should use ________ to hold the motorcycle while you start the engine and shift into first gear.
explanation: When starting on a hill, use the front brake to hold your motorcycle while you start the engine and shift into first gear. Change to the rear brake to hold your vehicle while you operate the throttle with your right hand. Slowly open the throttle for more power and slowly release the clutch. (If you release it too quickly, the front wheel may lift off the ground or the engine may stall.) Ease off the rear brake as the engine slows down and engages.

question: When you're preparing to pass the vehicle in front of you, you can increase your line of sight by riding in
explanation: To increase your line of sight when passing the vehicle ahead of you, drive in the left portion of your lane (left tire track position). This will let you see beyond the vehicle in front of you and spot oncoming hazards. It will also make your pass more visible to the driver of the vehicle.

question: When riding over metal gratings or rutted pavement, you should

answerOptions.text: stop and walk your motorcycle across.
keep a gentle but firm grip on the handlebars.

chosenAnswer: stop and walk your motorcycle across.
correctAnswer: keep a gentle but firm grip on the handlebars.
explanation: When you ride over grooved or rutted pavement or metal gratings, you may feel as though you are losing control of your motorcycle. This effect is greater on smaller vehicles than it is on larger ones; the smaller your vehicle, the larger the effect. Avoid overreacting. Keep a gentle but firm grip on the handlebars and drive as smoothly as you can, choosing the best route possible until you get back on a good surface.

question: When should a passenger get on a motorcycle?

answerOptions.text: After the engine has been started
Before the engine has been started
While it is still on the kickstand

chosenAnswer: After the engine has been started
correctAnswer: After the engine has been started
explanation: A passenger should get on a motorcycle only after the motorcyclist has mounted and started it.

question: A "blocking position"

answerOptions.text: encourages other drivers to share your lane.
discourages other drivers from sharing your lane.

correctAnswer: discourages other drivers from sharing your lane.
explanation: Usually the best lane position is a little to the left or right of the centre of your lane (left or right "tire track position"). It is also called the “blocking position” because it blocks or discourages other drivers from trying to squeeze past you in the same lane.

question: If you miss your exit on a highway, you should
explanation: If you miss your exit on a highway, don't stop, back up, or try to turn around. Doing so greatly increases the likelihood of a collision. Instead, take the next exit and re-enter the highway to return to the exit you missed.

question: A single broken yellow line usually marks the centreline of a two-way roadway where

answerOptions.text: driving on the other side of the line is allowed.
passing is allowed if the way is clear.

correctAnswer: passing is allowed if the way is clear.
explanation: A single broken yellow line usually marks the centreline of a two-way roadway on which a vehicle may pass if it is safe.

question: On a highway, when may you ride at the maximum speed limit?

answerOptions.text: Only if you can stop safely
On any highway designed for one-way traffic

chosenAnswer: Only if you can stop safely
correctAnswer: Only if you can stop safely
explanation: Obey the maximum speed limit posted on signs along the road, but always ride at a speed that will let you stop safely. This means riding below the maximum speed limit in bad weather, heavy traffic, or construction zones.

question: If your throttle is stuck and twisting it back and forth does not release it, you should
explanation: If your throttle is stuck and twisting it does not release it, operate the engine kill switch and pull in the clutch at the same time. This will cut power to the rear wheel.

question: Which of the following statements about railway crossings is FALSE?

answerOptions.text: Watch out for more than one train.
You can shift gears on the crossing.
Always stop when a train is close to the crossing.

correctAnswer: You can shift gears on the crossing.
explanation: Don't try to cross the tracks until you can cross them without stopping or shifting gears (because your vehicle might stall on the tracks). If the crossing gates are down, stop and remain stopped until the gates are raised. Always stop when a train is close to the crossing. A train cannot yield the right-of-way to you.

question: If the signal light changes from green to yellow (amber) as you approach the intersection, what should you do?

answerOptions.text: Stop. If a stop cannot be made safely, proceed with caution.
Continue through the intersection without slowing or stopping.
Sound your horn to warn pedestrians and other drivers that you do not intend to stop.
Speed up to clear the intersection as quickly as possible.

chosenAnswer: Stop. If a stop cannot be made safely, proceed with caution.
correctAnswer: Stop. If a stop cannot be made safely, proceed with caution.
explanation: If the signal light changes to yellow (amber) as you're approaching it, stop. Only if a stop cannot be made safely should you proceed with caution into the intersection.

question: You are in danger of hitting an object and need to swerve to avoid it. What is the best way to initiate a swerve?

answerOptions.text: Lean sharply in the opposite direction from which you need to swerve.
Apply a small amount of pressure to the handgrip on the side of your intended swerve.
Apply a small amount of pressure to the handgrip on the side opposite to your intended swerve.
Lean sharply in the direction in which you need to swerve.

chosenAnswer: Lean sharply in the opposite direction from which you need to swerve.
correctAnswer: Apply a small amount of pressure to the handgrip on the side of your intended swerve.
explanation: If you are in danger of hitting an object, the best way to initiate a swerve is to apply a small amount of pressure to the handgrip on the side of your intended swerve. This will cause the motorcycle to lean quickly in that direction.

question: While driving in the right lane, you see a bus leaving the bus bay with its left-turn signals flashing. You should

answerOptions.text: honk your horn and pass the bus quickly.
slow down or stop to yield the right-of-way to the bus.
speed up and pass the bus.

chosenAnswer: honk your horn and pass the bus quickly.
correctAnswer: slow down or stop to yield the right-of-way to the bus.
explanation: When a bus in a bus bay begins flashing its left-turn signals, indicating that it is ready to leave the bus bay, and you are approaching in the lane adjacent to the bus bay, you must allow the bus to re-enter traffic. Yielding the right-of-way to buses in this situation is the law.

question: If you need to shift gears as you approach a turn,

answerOptions.text: you do not need to shift gears for a turn.

explanation: If you need to shift gears as you approach a turn, you should do it before the turn if possible. This will help keep the turn smooth and stable. A sudden change in power to the rear wheel during a turn can cause a skid.

question: What can cause the engine to lock?
explanation: If an engine locks, it is likely low on oil. Always check your oil before beginning a trip. If needed, add oil as soon as possible or else the engine may seize.

question: You must NOT pass

answerOptions.text: when approaching a hill or curve where there is no clear visibility.
a school bus ahead with flashing red lights and an extended stop arm.
if there is a solid yellow line next to your lane.
in any of the above situations.

chosenAnswer: when approaching a hill or curve where there is no clear visibility.
correctAnswer: in any of the above situations.
explanation: A solid yellow line indicates a no-passing zone. You are not allowed to pass if there is a solid yellow line next to your lane. It is also illegal to pass a stopped school bus with flashing red lights and an extended stop arm. Also, do not pass when approaching a hill or curve where there is no clear visibility.

question: When driving at night, you should follow which of the following rules?
explanation: You are overdriving your headlight when you travel so fast that your stopping distance is farther than you can see with your headlight. This is a dangerous thing to do, because you are not giving yourself enough room to make a safe stop. In the dark, you have no way of knowing what lies beyond the reach of your headlight.

question: When passing a line of parked cars on your right, you should

answerOptions.text: ride close to the cars so their drivers can see you.
swerve from side to side so no one will try to share your lane.
ride in the left tire track position.

chosenAnswer: ride close to the cars so their drivers can see you.
correctAnswer: ride in the left tire track position.
explanation: When passing a line of parked cars on your right, you should ride in the left tire track position to be farthest away from hazards associated with parked cars.

question: When driving in fog, you should
explanation: Turn on your low-beam headlight whenever visibility is reduced, such as in bad weather. Don't use your high-beam headlight in bad weather. The bright light can reflect off fog, rain or snow and cause glare. However, if fog becomes so dense that you cannot see even 10 seconds ahead, pull off the road and stop in a safe place. Remember: If you can't see, you can't drive.

question: Which of the following statements are true about sharing the road with large trucks?

answerOptions.text: Stay in the left lane if a large truck ahead is making a left turn.
Large trucks have shorter stopping distances.
Large trucks create heavy turbulence that may affect your ability to control your motorcycle.
All of the above statements are true.

chosenAnswer: Stay in the left lane if a large truck ahead is making a left turn.
correctAnswer: Large trucks create heavy turbulence that may affect your ability to control your motorcycle.
explanation: A large vehicle can create heavy turbulence that can seriously affect your ability to control your vehicle when you are passing the larger one. If you must pass a large vehicle, try to relax and work with the air turbulence. Do not drive in the left lane when a large vehicle is making a left turn in front of you. Stay well back until the truck has cleared the turn, or else you might get squeezed between the truck and the curb. Keep in mind that large trucks require much longer stopping distances. When you pass a large truck, don't cut right in front of it. If you cut right in front of the truck and then have to slow down suddenly for some reason, the truck may not have enough space to avoid rear-ending you. Wait until you are well ahead of the truck before you merge back in front of it.

question: You have a green light and you want to go straight through the intersection, but other vehicles are blocking the intersection on the other side. What should you do?

answerOptions.text: Don't enter the intersection until the other vehicles have cleared it.
Merge into another lane and try to go around the other vehicles.
Enter the intersection and honk your horn to let the other drivers know you are there.
Enter the intersection and wait for the other vehicles to clear it.

chosenAnswer: Don't enter the intersection until the other vehicles have cleared it.
correctAnswer: Don't enter the intersection until the other vehicles have cleared it.
explanation: Never block an intersection. Even if the light is green, don't proceed into the intersection if you can't get through it. Instead, wait until the other side of the intersection is clear before you proceed.

question: To warn drivers that you are slowing down in an unusual place, you should
explanation: To warn drivers that you are slowing down in an unusual place, tap the brake pedal to flash your brake light to the driver behind you.

question: An accident must be reported to the police if

answerOptions.text: someone has been hurt or killed.
any of the above are true.

chosenAnswer: someone has been hurt or killed.
correctAnswer: any of the above are true.
explanation: By law, you must report to the police any collision involving injuries or damage to vehicles or property exceeding $2,000.

question: Unless otherwise posted, the maximum speed limit on a highway outside of a city, town or village is
explanation: Where there are no posted speed limits, the maximum speed limit is 50 km/h in cities, towns and villages, and 80 km/h elsewhere.

question: If you fail to present your insurance card when stopped by a police officer, you may be fined
explanation: When driving your own vehicle or someone else’s, you must carry the pink liability insurance card from the insurance company for that particular vehicle. You must show this card if a police officer asks for it. If you do not, you may be fined up to $400.

question: While an M1 licence holder is operating a motorcycle, his or her blood alcohol level must not be greater than
explanation: M1 licence holders may not operate a motorcycle if they have been drinking alcohol.

question: You must report a change of name or address to the Ministry of Transportation within
explanation: If your name or address changes, it is your responsibility to report it to the Ministry of Transportation within six days.

question: If you are convicted of _________, six demerit points will be added to your driving record.

answerOptions.text: driving at 50 km/h or more over the speed limit
failing to stop for a school bus

explanation: If you are convicted of careless driving, racing, failing to stop for a school bus or exceeding the speed limit by 50 km/hour or more, six demerit points will be added to your driving record. You may also face additional penalties.

question: If you are found guilty of falsifying your motorcycle's insurance documents, you may face which of the following penalties?

answerOptions.text: Loss of licence for up to a year
Loss of your vehicle for up to 3 months
One or more of the above

chosenAnswer: Loss of licence for up to a year
correctAnswer: One or more of the above
explanation: Before you may register your motorcycle or moped or renew your registration, you must show proof that you have insurance coverage. If you provide false documents or lie about your insurance, you can be fined from $5,000 to $25,000. You may also lose your licence for up to one year and have your motorcycle or moped taken away for up to three months.

question: If you are found guilty of driving around a railway crossing barrier, ____ demerit point(s) will be added to your driving record.
explanation: Many different offences carry three points each. These include driving through, around or under a railway crossing barrier; failing to yield the right-of-way; and failing to obey a stop sign, traffic light or railway crossing signal.

question: If your licence is suspended,

answerOptions.text: you may operate a motorcycle in an emergency.
you may operate a motorcycle when accompanied by a licensed rider.
you may not operate a motorcycle.
you may only ride to and from work.

chosenAnswer: you may operate a motorcycle in an emergency.
correctAnswer: you may not operate a motorcycle.
explanation: If your licence is suspended, you may not operate a motorcycle under any circumstances. [R.S.O. 1990, c. H.8, s. 36]

question: Which of the following statements about a Safety Standards Certificate are true?

answerOptions.text: It is a document that certifies your vehicle's fitness.
It is illegal to ride your motorcycle without it.
It is valid for 36 days after the inspection.
All of the above statements are correct.

chosenAnswer: It is a document that certifies your vehicle's fitness.
correctAnswer: All of the above statements are correct.
explanation: An inspection station licensed by the Ministry of Transportation to inspect motorcycles can issue a safety standards certificate (SSC) for your vehicle, provided that it has passed an inspection. An SSC is valid for 36 days after the inspection.

question: __________ can affect your ability to ride a motorcycle.
explanation: Not only alcohol can affect your ability to ride a motorcycle. Some illegal, prescription, and over-the-counter drugs can also affect your riding abilities. If you're not sure of the effects of a particular medication, check the warning label or ask your pharmacist or doctor.

question: Class M1 and M2 drivers will be sent a second warning letter encouraging you to improve your driving behaviour if they have accumulated
explanation: See section “Demerit-point System for Level 1 and Level 2 Drivers” in the official MTO Driver's handbook. Note that this question refers to Level 1 and Level 2 drivers, NOT fully licensed drivers.

question: According to the Official Ministry of Transportation Motorcycle Handbook, how often should you inspect your motorcycle for problems?
explanation: Motorcycles require more maintenance than cars. It is important that you read your owner’s manual, inspect your motorcycle carefully and fix things right away. According to the Official Ministry of Transportation Motorcycle Handbook, in addition to the check you do each time you ride, some things as tires, wheels, coolant, battery, drive line, brakes and shock absorbers should be carefully examined weekly.

question: When are you allowed to lend your licence to a friend?

answerOptions.text: If your friend is borrowing your motorcycle

explanation: It is illegal to lend your licence to anyone or use someone else's licence as your own.

question: Unless otherwise posted, the maximum speed limit in cities, towns and villages is
explanation: Where there are no posted speed limits, the maximum speed limit is 50 km/h in cities, towns and villages, and 80 km/h elsewhere.

question: How many passengers can an M1 driver carry?
explanation: M1 drivers are not allowed to carry any passengers.

question: If you change the colour of your motorcycle, you must

answerOptions.text: report it to the police within 6 days.
report it to a Driver and Vehicle Licence Issuing Office within 6 days.

chosenAnswer: report it to the police within 6 days.
correctAnswer: report it to a Driver and Vehicle Licence Issuing Office within 6 days.
explanation: Your vehicle permit includes a description of your motorcycle. If you have changed anything, such as the colour, you must report it to a Driver and Vehicle Licence Issuing Office within six days.

question: An M1 licence is valid for
explanation: Your M1 licence is only valid for 90 days, so it is a good idea to book your Level One road test early so you can complete it before your M1 licence expires.

question: To legally drive a motorcycle, the driver must

answerOptions.text: have an M1, M2 or M licence.
meet all of the above requirements.

chosenAnswer: have an M1, M2 or M licence.
correctAnswer: meet all of the above requirements.
explanation: To legally drive a motorcycle in Ontario, you must be at least 16 years old and have a valid motorcycle licence, and a licence plate must be attached to the rear of your vehicle.

question: While you hold an M1 licence, you may NOT drive

answerOptions.text: from one hour after sunset to one hour before sunrise.
from one-half hour after sunset to one-half hour before sunrise.

correctAnswer: from one-half hour after sunset to one-half hour before sunrise.
explanation: While you hold an M1 licence, you may NOT drive from one-half hour after sunset to one-half hour before sunrise. There are also other restrictions on your driving.

question: What does this road sign mean?
explanation: This sign indicates a traffic control person ahead. Traffic control persons (also known as flag persons) may direct traffic at construction (work) zones by using hand signals and STOP/SLOW paddles. Slow down and follow all directions of the traffic control person.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: This lane exits to the right.
There is a slight bend or curve in the road ahead.
Keep right if there is faster traffic.
There is a sharp bend or curve in the road ahead.

chosenAnswer: This lane exits to the right.
correctAnswer: There is a slight bend or curve in the road ahead.
explanation: This sign warns of a slight bend or curve in the road ahead.

question: What does this road sign mean?
explanation: This is a “Bumpy or Uneven Pavement Ahead” road sign. Slow down.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: You must be driving a school bus to enter this area.
School buses load or unload in this area without using the flashing red lights or the stop arm.
School buses load or unload in this area by turning on the flashing red lights and extending the stop arm.

chosenAnswer: You must be driving a school bus to enter this area.
correctAnswer: School buses load or unload in this area without using the flashing red lights or the stop arm.
explanation: This sign marks a loading zone for school buses. In this area, school buses load or unload passengers without using the flashing red lights or the stop arm.

question: What does this sign mean?

answerOptions.text: Slight bend or curve in the road ahead

explanation: This sign means that there is a closed lane ahead. If you're in that lane, adjust your speed to merge with traffic in the lane indicated by the arrow.

question: What does this construction sign mean?

answerOptions.text: Slow down and obey the flagger's directions.
The pavement is milled or grooved.
There is road work operation ahead.

chosenAnswer: Slow down and obey the flagger's directions.
correctAnswer: There is road work operation ahead.
explanation: This is a “Road Work Ahead” sign.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: You may park in the designated area during the posted times.
You may not park in the designated area during the posted times.

chosenAnswer: You may park in the designated area during the posted times.
correctAnswer: You may park in the designated area during the posted times.
explanation: A green circle indicates permission. This regulatory sign means that you may park in the designated area for two hours during the posted times.

question: What does this sign mean?
explanation: This warning sign indicates that the road ahead is divided by a median. Keep right of it.

question: What does this road sign mean?
explanation: This warning sign is a "School Crossing Ahead" road sign. Follow the directions of the crossing guard or school safety patroller.

question: What does this road sign mean?
explanation: This is a “Steep Hill Ahead” road sign. This sign says that you are about to enter a steep 12% descent. Check your brakes. To reduce wear on your brakes, shift into a lower gear even if your vehicle has an automatic transmission.

question: What does this road sign mean?
explanation: This is a sign indicating that a railway crossing is ahead. Slow down, watch for trains and be prepared to stop if a train is approaching.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: No bicycles allowed on this road

correctAnswer: No bicycles allowed on this road
explanation: A red slash inside a red circle means "no." This regulatory sign means that bicycles are not allowed.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: This lane is for bicycles only.
This road is an official bicycle route.
There is a bicycle crossing ahead.
No pedestrians are allowed on this road.

chosenAnswer: This lane is for bicycles only.
correctAnswer: This road is an official bicycle route.
explanation: This road is an official bicycle route. Watch for cyclists and be prepared to share the road with them.

question: What does this sign mean?

answerOptions.text: When a school bus stops with its red lights flashing and its stop sign arm swung out, you may proceed if you're on the opposite side of the road.
When a school bus stops with its red lights flashing and its stop sign arm swung out, you must stop at least 20 metres from the bus.
When a school bus stops with its red lights flashing and its stop sign arm swung out, you must stop at any point before reaching the bus.
When a school bus stops with its red lights flashing and its stop sign arm swung out, you must slow down to 25 km/h and proceed with caution.

chosenAnswer: When a school bus stops with its red lights flashing and its stop sign arm swung out, you may proceed if you're on the opposite side of the road.
correctAnswer: When a school bus stops with its red lights flashing and its stop sign arm swung out, you must stop at least 20 metres from the bus.
explanation: When a school bus stops with its red lights flashing and its stop sign arm extended, you must stop at least 20 metres from the bus, regardless of which side of the road the bus is on. Remain stopped until the stop sign arm is retracted and the red lights stop flashing. (The only exception is if the bus is stopped on the opposite side of a divided highway. In that case, you don't have to stop for it.)

question: What does this sign mean?
explanation: This emblem on the rear of the vehicle identifies it as a slow-moving vehicle (one whose speed is 40 km/h or less). Slow-moving vehicles include farm equipment and some construction equipment. If you see this emblem on a vehicle you are following, slow down and be patient.

question: A sign with a green circle

answerOptions.text: indicates a route for large trucks.

explanation: A regulatory sign with a green circle is a permissive sign. It means you may or must do the activity shown inside the circle.

question: What does this road sign mean?
explanation: This is a “Bicycle Crossing Ahead” road sign.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: Maximum safe speed on the ramp
Maximum safe speed for the curve
Maximum speed limit on the highway

chosenAnswer: Maximum safe speed on the ramp
correctAnswer: Maximum speed limit on the highway
explanation: This is a “Maximum Speed Limit on the Highway” road sign.

question: What does this road sign mean?
explanation: This is a “Deer Crossing” road sign. Watch for deer and be prepared to stop for them.

question: What does this road sign mean?
explanation: This is a “Watch for Falling Rocks” road sign. Drive with extra caution and be prepared to avoid a collision.

question: What does this sign mean?

answerOptions.text: Shows distances to towns or cities
Shows directions to nearby towns or cities
Shows the exits on the upcoming roundabout and where they will take you

chosenAnswer: Shows distances to towns or cities
correctAnswer: Shows the exits on the upcoming roundabout and where they will take you
explanation: This sign shows the exits on the upcoming roundabout and where they will take you. After you pass this sign and approach the roundabout, you should also encounter a roundabout warning sign.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: No stopping for more than 3 minutes
No smoking for more than 3 minutes
Idling permitted for at least 3 minutes
No idling for more than 3 minutes

chosenAnswer: No stopping for more than 3 minutes
correctAnswer: No idling for more than 3 minutes
explanation: This type of sign is a “No Idling” sign. This particular sign means that you may not idle your engine here for more than 3 minutes.

question: What does this road sign mean?
explanation: A red slash inside a red circle means "no." This regulatory sign means that cars and other vehicles should not pass.

question: What does this sign mean?

answerOptions.text: A traffic control person is ahead. Drive slowly and watch for instructions.
Watch for pedestrians and be prepared to share the road with them.
A survey crew is working on the road ahead.

correctAnswer: A survey crew is working on the road ahead.
explanation: This sign is seen at construction zones where survey crews may be working. Proceed with caution.

question: What does this road sign mean?
explanation: This is a pedestrian crossover. Be prepared to stop and yield the right-of-way to pedestrians. You may not pass within 30 metres of a pedestrian crossover.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: The speed limit is 40 km/h at all times.
During school hours when the yellow lights are flashing, the speed limit is 40 km/h.

chosenAnswer: The speed limit is 40 km/h at all times.
correctAnswer: During school hours when the yellow lights are flashing, the speed limit is 40 km/h.
explanation: During school hours when the yellow lights are flashing, you must always follow the speed limit shown. This sign means that the speed limit when the yellow lights are flashing is 40 km/h.

question: What does this road sign mean?

answerOptions.text: Danger; there is a sharp turn ahead.
There is a playground to the right.

correctAnswer: Danger; there is a sharp turn ahead.
explanation: This sign warns of a sudden turn in the road ahead in the direction shown by the arrow. Slow down and proceed with care.

question: What does this road sign mean?
